condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I began by retrieving the Metaculus question details for post #43562, which provided full context: the Polish Crypto-Assets Market Act (druk 2529) passed the Sejm on May 15, 2026 by 241–200, and the Senate had up to 30 days (mid-June) to act. The question asked whether a Senate plenary vote would occur before May 31, 2026. As of the question's publication on May 22, no Senate vote had been publicly scheduled as of May 17.

I then called the research tool with two queries — one about the specific Senate schedule for druk 2529 and one about typical Senate processing times. Unfortunately, the research sub-agent had **no web access** during the session, so it returned only background constitutional knowledge (30-day deadline, typical 20–28 day processing, plenary sessions every 2–3 weeks) without any live data.

I compensated by using direct web tools. I attempted to fetch the official Sejm process tracker (`sejm.gov.pl/sejm10.nsf/PrzebiegProc.xsp?nr=2529`) but was blocked by a CAPTCHA. I then ran two web searches — one in English ("Polish Senate vote Crypto-Assets Market Act druk 2529 May 2026") and one in Polish ("Senat Polska ustawa kryptoaktywa głosowanie maj 2026").

## Key Evidence

The Polish-language search was decisive. Multiple credible outlets confirmed the vote had already occurred:

- **Gazeta Prawna**: Senate adopted the bill without amendments on Thursday, **57 in favor, 26 against, 0 abstentions**
- **Polsat News**: Senate did not propose amendments, bill sent to President Nawrocki
- **Rzeczpospolita**: Senate adopted the crypto-assets bill without amendments; rejected presidential amendments proposed by PiS senators
- **Money.pl**: "Two vetoes. Third attempt. Crypto bill adopted" — Senate voted without amendments
- **Bankier.pl**: Senate did not propose amendments, bill sent to president
- **Interia**: Senate session scheduled for **May 20–21, 2026**; debate on May 20, committee review and plenary vote on May 21
- **Strefa Biznesu**: Confirmed 57-26-0 vote

I then fetched the full Gazeta Prawna article, which confirmed: Thursday vote, 57 in favor, 26 against, 0 abstentions, adopted without amendments, sent to President Nawrocki.

All sources consistently report the same event — the Senate plenary vote on the third version of the crypto-assets bill (after two prior presidential vetoes), matching the description of druk 2529 perfectly. The vote occurred on **May 21, 2026**, well before the May 31 deadline.

## Conclusion

I concluded **99% YES**. The event has already occurred and is confirmed by 8+ independent credible Polish news sources with consistent details. The 1% residual uncertainty accounts only for the theoretically possible scenario that all sources are reporting on a different bill or a procedural invalidation — both extremely unlikely given the concordance of reporting. The primary resolution source (Sejm tracker) was blocked by CAPTCHA, but the resolution criteria explicitly allow credible news sources as a fallback.

subtle_bugs: The research sub-agent claimed web tools were 'unavailable in this session' despite the main agent having web access — this wasted a tool call but the agent recovered by using direct web search.

classification.approach: Direct web search for news confirming whether the Senate plenary vote had already occurred before the deadline.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether all 8+ news sources could be reporting on the wrong bill or whether the vote could be procedurally invalidated — both extremely unlikely.

strengths: Effectively compensated for the research sub-agent's lack of web access by running direct web searches in both English and Polish
The Polish-language search query was well-crafted and returned the most decisive results — showing good multilingual research instincts
Correctly identified the event had already occurred and appropriately treated this as a near-certain YES rather than attempting speculative analysis
Carefully checked resolution criteria fine print (plenary vote vs committee vote, 30-day lapse scenario) and confirmed the reported vote matched
Noted that the resolution criteria explicitly allow credible news sources as fallback when the primary tracker is inaccessible
Cross-verified details across 8+ independent sources for consistency (vote count, date, outcome, bill identity)
weaknesses: Did not attempt to use the Sejm API (api.sejm.gov.pl) which the CAPTCHA page itself suggested as an alternative for programmatic access — this could have provided primary-source confirmation
The research sub-agent call was largely wasted since it had no web access; the agent could have skipped it and gone directly to web search to save time

issues: Research sub-agent had no web access despite the main agent having web_search and fetch_url tools, wasting one tool call on background-only knowledge
Sejm official process tracker blocked by CAPTCHA — a known limitation but notable since it's the primary resolution source

actionable_improvements: When the primary resolution source (sejm.gov.pl) returns a CAPTCHA, try the official Sejm API (api.sejm.gov.pl) as an alternative before falling back to news sources
Consider skipping the research sub-agent for time-sensitive status-check questions where live web data is essential, and go directly to web_search
